notes: Real on-screen Oxide label scene rendered through the live MetalView host path.
Refresh mode: native
Device executable: `OxideHost`
Metric scope: live on-screen Oxide host workload running through the real MetalView app path on the physical iPhone.

Clock metric scope: promoted `signpost_frame_present_s` into `clock_s` for the official matched on-screen Oxide/UIKit comparison; raw end-to-end Oxide host workload wall clock remains under `workload_s`.
Capture source: on-screen Oxide benchmark summary emitted through the parked device app console log.

GPU trace source: collected through a separate launched Metal trace after the console-summary on-screen Oxide run, so in-app renderer stage summaries remain available when xctrace target stdout is empty.
GPU counter status: the launched device trace rejected the Metal GPU Counters profile, so this case was retried with direct GPU time and GPU latency only.

GPU interval attribution status: this trace exposed no direct target-process GPU intervals, so GPU time and latency were summarized from compositor-inclusive Metal GPU intervals within the same trace window.
GPU compositor detail: the compositor-inclusive overlap was dominated by `` (6009 rows, 392.823 ms total across 4 workload window(s)).

GPU metric semantics: `gpu_time_s` is the total overlapping Metal GPU execution duration inside each bounded workload window. `gpu_latency_s` is the mean CPU-to-GPU latency across overlapping Metal GPU intervals inside that same window.
In-app GPU timing: Oxide host console stage summaries provided Metal command-buffer and timestamp-counter timings; process-scoped Metal System Trace remains the external cross-check.
Trace process identity: used `Oxide Demo` from the saved Metal trace instead of executable `OxideHost` when reducing workload windows.
Frame cadence source: device console CADisplayLink summary emitted by the measured XCTest workload window.
Headline metric: `signpost_frame_present_s`.

Device executable: `OxideHost`

Clock metric scope: promoted `signpost_frame_present_s` into `clock_s` for the official matched on-screen Oxide/UIKit comparison; raw end-to-end Oxide host workload wall clock remains under `workload_s`.
Capture source: on-screen Oxide benchmark summary emitted through the parked device app console log.

GPU trace source: collected through a separate launched Metal trace after the console-summary on-screen Oxide run, so in-app renderer stage summaries remain available when xctrace target stdout is empty.
GPU counter status: the launched device trace rejected the Metal GPU Counters profile, so this case was retried with direct GPU time and GPU latency only.

GPU interval attribution status: this trace exposed no direct target-process GPU intervals, so GPU time and latency were summarized from compositor-inclusive Metal GPU intervals within the same trace window.
GPU compositor detail: the compositor-inclusive overlap was dominated by `` (2231 rows, 165.205 ms total across 4 workload window(s)).

GPU metric semantics: `gpu_time_s` is the total overlapping Metal GPU execution duration inside each bounded workload window. `gpu_latency_s` is the mean CPU-to-GPU latency across overlapping Metal GPU intervals inside that same window.
In-app GPU timing: Oxide host console stage summaries provided Metal command-buffer and timestamp-counter timings; process-scoped Metal System Trace remains the external cross-check.
Trace process identity: used `Oxide Demo` from the saved Metal trace instead of executable `OxideHost` when reducing workload windows.

layers.label: Oxide On-Screen Host Battery
notes: This device report is captured through the real on-screen Oxide MetalView host path instead of the offscreen Rust perf runner.

layers.label: Workspace Engine Battery
notes: The broader offscreen engine and microbenchmark suite remains in benchmarks/workspace and is intentionally not mixed into this device comparison report.

battery.label: Launch & Lifecycle
notes: The on-screen device battery does not yet persist launch, resume, or foreground lifecycle rows; those remain covered by the separate workspace battery.

battery.label: Primitive Mount / Update / Destroy
notes: The on-screen device battery carries headline component encode rows, but does not yet include the full mount/update/destroy lifecycle matrix on physical hardware.

battery.label: Layout & Invalidation
notes: Dedicated physical-device layout and invalidation rows are not yet part of this report.

battery.label: Text & Text Input
notes: The device battery has text-focus response coverage, but not the full keystroke, paste, selection, IME, and cache-state text-input matrix.

battery.label: Image Pipeline
notes: The on-screen device battery includes the custom camera preview path, while decode/upload/first-visible image rows remain in the separate workspace battery.

battery.label: Lists, Grids, & Chat
notes: Collection component and journey rows exist, but the physical-device report does not yet persist the full feed, grid, and chat scroll matrix.

battery.label: Navigation & Input Latency
notes: The official matched device battery carries direct Oxide navigation/input response workloads through the live host path.

battery.label: Animation & Visual Effects
notes: Representative animation rows exist, but hitch-ratio and refresh-mode matrices are still not persisted as first-class device rows.

battery.label: State Mutation & Reconciliation
notes: Single-node, percentage, and full-theme reconciliation rows are not yet captured in the physical-device Oxide report.

battery.label: OS Bridge Overhead
notes: Permission, sensor, import, share, and localhost bridge rows are not yet captured in the physical-device Oxide report.

battery.label: Endurance, Memory, & Thermal Drift
notes: Open/close, tab-switch, idle-animation endurance, and thermal drift rows are not yet captured in the physical-device Oxide report.

battery.label: Stress & Pathological Regressions
notes: Static-idle and renderer scene rows exist, but the full pathological 10k-node, animation, and ticker traps are not yet captured in the physical-device Oxide report.

battery.label: Representative Journeys
notes: Representative Oxide journeys are captured through the live MetalView host path, but this is tracked separately from the canonical workload-family rows above.

battery.label: Renderer Scene GPU Paths
notes: Dedicated renderer rows for damage prefiltering, static idle, and nine-slice composition are captured through the live host path.

battery.label: Camera Preview
notes: The official custom-camera row uses the real on-screen Oxide preview path with Oxide owning the visible preview on the phone.
Device: `Victor’s iPhone`
Executable: `OxideHost`

Device flow: launch the parked host app on the physical iPhone with a live on-screen Oxide workload selected, collect workload and memory summaries from the app console, and collect direct GPU/signpost metrics from a process-scoped launched Metal System Trace when tracing is enabled.

Fairness scope: headline rows use visible workload, transition, interaction, or present signposts; app-process CPU and memory are attribution metrics, not a claim that all iOS framework or compositor work is charged to the app process.
Comparison scope: only on-screen Oxide host cases are persisted here. Offscreen Rust workspace numbers remain separate and are not part of the official device comparison.

findings.summary: This device report measures the live on-screen Oxide host path rather than the offscreen Rust perf runner, so it is the authoritative Oxide side of the official device comparison.
